questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to promote the welfare of the people?
What is the minimum age required to contest elections to Panchayats as per Article 243F?
Which of the following parts of the Indian Constitution deals with financial relations between the Centre and the States?
Which of the following Parts of the Constitution was amended by the 97ᵗʰ Amendment to promote co-operative societies?
Which of the following Amendments added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble?
How are the one-third reserved seats for women distributed across different categories in local bodies?
questions.option_1: Only one-third of the general (unreserved) seats are reserved for women, excluding reserved-category seats.
questions.option_2: One-third reservation applies only to leadership positions such as Sarpanch or Chairperson.
questions.option_3: One-third of all seats, including those reserved for SC/ST/OBC categories, are reserved for women, allowing a seat to be reserved simultaneously for a woman and for an SC/ST/OBC candidate.
questions.option_4: One-third reservation applies only to rural Panchayats and not to urban municipal bodies.

questions.question: According to the Citizenship Act, how many years must a person ordinarily reside in India to apply for citizenship by naturalisation under general conditions?
What makes basic rules in the Constitution effective?
questions.option_4: They are based on popular customs only.

questions.question: Which of the following Amendments clarified that the President must act in accordance with the advice of the Council of Ministers?
Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution provides for the composition of Municipalities?
The Immigration and Foreigners Bill 2025, repeals which of the following Acts?
Which body acts as the apex at the district level in Panchayati Raj?
Why is legal enforceability of rules important in a society?
questions.option_1: So people can break them without fear
questions.option_2: It provides assurance that others will follow the rules.
questions.option_3: It gives power to the police only.

questions.question: Which of the following Directive Principles of State Policy was added by the Forty-fourth Amendment Act, 1978?
questions.option_1: Reduction of inequalities in income and status among individuals and groups
questions.option_2: Protection and improvement of the environment and wildlife
questions.option_3: Provision for free legal aid to the poor
questions.option_4: Participation of workers in industrial management

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes between the Cabinet and the Council of Ministers?
questions.option_1: The Cabinet is not part of the executive, but the Council is part of the executive
questions.option_2: The Cabinet is larger and includes all ministers, while the Council is a smaller group
questions.option_3: The Cabinet includes senior ministers, while the Council includes all categories of ministers
questions.option_4: The Council of Ministers does not include the Prime Minister

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution defines the money bill?
Which level of government generally legislates on matters such as police, public health and agriculture in India?
Which of the following Articles deals with the citizenship of people of Indian origin residing outside India?
Which of the following is a discretionary power of the President of India?
questions.option_1: Dissolving the Lok Sabha without advice
questions.option_2: Giving assent to a Money Bill
questions.option_3: Appointing the Chief Justice of India
questions.option_4: Appointing a Prime Minister when no party has a clear majority

questions.question: Article 6 of the Constitution pertains to the citizenship of persons who migrated to India from which of the following territories?

Article 323B of the Constitution of India (added by the 42nd Amendment) empowers the Parliament to establish tribunals for adjudication of matters related to certain subjects. Which of the following matters is specifically covered under Article 323B?
questions.option_2: Only industrial disputes between employers and workers
questions.option_3: Recruitment and service conditions of public servants (civil services)
questions.option_4: Matters such as taxation, foreign exchange, land reforms, etc., which can be assigned to tribunals by legislation

questions.question: Which of the following types of cases is NOT generally taken up by Lok Adalats in India?
The 25ᵗʰ Amendment curtailed the right to property by amending which Article of Indian Constitution?
After its 1971 electoral victory, Indira Gandhi’s government amended the Constitution to remove legal obstacles to abolishing which of the following privileges?
questions.option_1: Privy purses of former princely rulers

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Gram Sabha is correct?
questions.option_1: It meets only once every five years.
questions.option_2: It consists only of elected members of the Gram Panchayat.
questions.option_3: It has authority to override the decisions of the Panchayat.
questions.option_4: It comprises all adult voters in the Panchayat area; its role is defined by state law.
